title: gymnanthemum coursii
intro: Gymnanthemum coursii, also known as African bush tea or bush tea, is a plant species native to southern Africa, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, headache, and respiratory issues, due to its high concentration of flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Gymnanthemum coursii is often prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the dried leaves in hot water, which is said to provide a soothing and refreshing beverage.
